## Commercial usage for real projects

NA-AI Landing is a paid template with commercial usage rights depending on the license included with your purchase.

The template is designed for:

- personal projects
- commercial products
- SaaS marketing pages
- AI product launches
- agency delivery
- client work
- startup validation

<DocsUpgradeCTA
  title="Use the template to ship products, not resell the source."
  description="NA-AI Landing is licensed for building and launching real projects while protecting the source code from redistribution as a competing product."
  productSlug="na-ai-landing"
  buyLabel="Get NA-AI Landing — €49"
  secondaryHref="/license"
  secondaryLabel="PyColors license"
/>

<Callout title="Core idea">
  You can use the template to build and ship projects. You cannot resell, redistribute, or publish the source code as a competing template.
</Callout>

## Common license scope

Always review the `LICENSE` file included in your purchased package.

Common license models may include:

- single-project license
- multi-project license
- extended commercial license

The governing terms are:

- the LICENSE file delivered with the ZIP package
- the legal pages published on PyColors

<Callout type="info" title="Legal rule">
  If there is any difference between summaries and the shipped LICENSE file, the LICENSE file governs usage.
</Callout>

## Allowed vs not allowed

<PreferAvoid
  preferTitle="Generally allowed"
  avoidTitle="Not allowed"

## What the license does not include

NA-AI Landing does not include:

- backend implementation
- authentication
- database foundations
- Stripe billing
- CMS integration
- email infrastructure
- custom implementation work
- guaranteed third-party service configuration

If you need:

- authentication
- billing
- protected routes
- backend-ready SaaS foundations

review Starter Pro.

<Callout title="Product boundary">
  NA-AI Landing is the frontend marketing layer. It is not a complete SaaS application.
</Callout>

### Support scope

Official support focuses on:

- package access
- installation questions
- setup clarification
- documentation clarification
- delivered source code issues

Support generally does not include:

- building custom backends
- rewriting the design for your brand
- integrating third-party services for you
- creating custom features
- debugging unrelated infrastructure

### Versioning policy

NA-AI Landing follows Semantic Versioning.

| Version | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| Patch `1.0.x` | Bug fixes, visual polish, and minor improvements |
| Minor `1.x.0` | New sections, components, or backward-compatible additions |
| Major `x.0.0` | Breaking changes affecting structure, configuration, or architecture |

<Callout title="Versioning principle">
  Semantic Versioning helps keep updates predictable as the template evolves.
</Callout>

## Common questions

<Accordions type="single">
  <Accordion title="Can I use NA-AI Landing for client work?">
    Yes, if your license allows commercial and client usage. Review the LICENSE file included after purchase.
  </Accordion>

  <Accordion title="Can I resell the template after modifying it?">
    No. You cannot resell, redistribute, sublicense, or publish the source code as a competing template.
  </Accordion>

  <Accordion title="Can I connect it to my backend?">
    Yes. You can connect the frontend to your own backend, CMS, analytics, form provider, or payment flow.
  </Accordion>

  <Accordion title="Does the template include Stripe payments?">
    No. NA-AI Landing is frontend-only. Use Starter Pro if you need billing foundations.
  </Accordion>
</Accordions>

## Practical usage examples

Examples that are generally acceptable depending on the license:

- launching your SaaS marketing site
- building a startup landing page
- shipping a client website
- adapting the visuals and branding
- integrating APIs and analytics
- deploying the final website publicly

Examples that are generally not allowed:

- selling the template source code
- republishing the ZIP package
- distributing the source publicly
- turning the source into a competing template business

<Callout title="Ship the project, not the source as a product.">
  The license is designed to help you build real products while protecting the template from redistribution.
</Callout>
